5601: Wadding of textile materials and articles thereof; textile fibres, not exceeding 5 mm in length (flock), textile dust and mill neps
56012: Wadding of textile materials and articles thereof These subheadings include ‘cotton buds’, sticks of wood, plastics or rolled paper having at one or both ends a ‘bud’ of wadding used for cleaning ears, nostrils, nails, etc., for applying antiseptics or skin lotions, and in beauty care.
56012110: Absorbent The explanatory note on the term ‘absorbent’ at subheading 5201 00 10 applies, mutatis mutandis.
56013000: Textile flock and dust and mill neps See the HS Explanatory Note to heading 5601, (B) and (C).
5602: Felt, whether or not impregnated, coated, covered or laminated
5602101: Needleloom felt See the HS Explanatory Note to heading 5602, fourth para graph.
5602103: Stitch-bonded fibre fabrics See the HS Explanatory Note to heading 5602, seventh paragraph.
5603: Nonwovens, whether or not impregnated, coated, covered or laminated This heading includes products where a nonwoven web (of textile fibres) is combined with fibres of cellulose by spun lacing/hydroentangling bonding process (see also the HSEN to heading 5603, II. Bonding and III. Finishing). On one side the nonwoven web remains predominant/ visible and on the other side the cellulose fibres remain predominant/visible. Considering the manufacturing process and the characteris tics given to the product by the textile fibres (strength, resistance, long-lasting properties, softness on the skin, liquid repellent) the presence of the cellulose fibres does not change the character of the product. Even if the textile fibres are not in all cases predominant, this type of product remain classified in this heading. See also the explanatory note to subheading 6210 10 92. CHAPTER 57 CARPETS AND OTHER TEXTILE FLOOR COVERINGS General For the classification, within headings, of articles composed of two or more textile materials, see the general explanatory note to this section.
560600: Gimped yarn, and strip and the like of heading 5404 or 5405, gimped (other than those of heading 5605 and gimped horsehair yarn); chenille yarn (including flock chenille yarn); loop wale-yarn
56060091: Gimped yarn The core of a gimped yarn may consist also of an elas tomeric yarn (see note 13 to this section).
